manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 hooks, 1 flower, 1 card, 1 eraser, 1 sponge

Your secret value function is:

hook: 3, flower: 3, card: 0, eraser: 1, sponge: 0

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.

message.content: I propose we split the items like this: I take the two hooks and the flower, you take the card, eraser, and sponge. Does that work for you?

We have limited messages: maximum of 5 messages each, or can terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.

We must not include square brackets in regular messages, as those would be interpreted as secret proposals. The secret proposal must be formatted exactly: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]" like [Proposal: 2 hooks, 1 flower] etc.

The game is cooperative, but the objective is to maximize sum of both scores, not just your own.

Our values: H=3, F=3, C=0, E=1, S=0. So we want H and F. Possibly also E.

So we can propose to split them, maybe each gets one hook, and the flower goes to me (or them). The leftover items may have low values for us, but maybe they have positive for them? Not sure.

However we must eventually submit a secret proposal that matches complementarity: both proposals must be complementary, meaning there are enough items to fulfill both proposals. But it's not required that we both propose exactly complementary; they might propose something else, and if they are complementary (i.e., combined they don't exceed any item count), then both get scores, otherwise both get zero. So seems many splits yield total 10 because total value of items is sum of values for each item (if we allocate items according to value). You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 hooks, 1 flower, 1 card, 1 eraser, 1 sponge

Your secret value function is:

hook: 3, flower: 3, card: 0, eraser: 1, sponge: 0

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
I propose we split the items like this: I take the two hooks and the flower, you take the card, eraser, and sponge. Does that work for you?
I propose we split the items like this: I take the eraser and the sponge, you take the two hooks, the flower, and the card. Does that work for you?
That works for me. I'll take the two hooks, the flower, and the card, and you take the eraser and the sponge. Let me confirm this is our agreement.
The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
